question: What should I conclude when LumenLoop person-entity lookup succeeds but every content collection is empty?

golden.answer: Treat this person-entity result as a successful soft-empty lookup: no indexed articles, AV, events, proposals, or SCF submissions were linked to that exact person entity. It is not an outage and not proof the person has no real-world footprint. Report the index-scoped absence and, if useful, retry exact-name variants or broader content search.
keyFacts: Recognizes the person entity envelope can succeed while all content arrays are empty.
Scopes the conclusion to no linked content in the LumenLoop index.
avoid: Do NOT claim the person does not exist or has no public work.
Do NOT convert empty arrays into a transport or authorization error.
golden.notes: Dedicated person entity empty catalog-note trap; NEG / soft-empty lane.

sources.note: Fetch-verified local path exists and defines the exercised contract/workflow.

evidence: 2026-07-11 localhost:8787 search→execute probe observed: HTTP 200; find_content_by_entity for an unknown person returned envelope ok=true with empty articles, av, events, proposals, and scf_submissions arrays.
catalog/manifest.json exists and supports the behavioral golden.
Sibling sweep: reviewed existing battery cases sharing the exercised surface; no contradictory behavioral golden found.
